content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 13, 1999


Barbara Aguire
ORO Financial of California, Inc.
2051 East Shaw Avenue
Fresno, California 93710

Re: Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-99-219

Dear Ms. Aguire:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

     May a candidate use campaign funds to lease a portion of a building for his or her
campaign headquarters when the building is owned by a corporation in which the candidate is the
sole shareholder?

CONCLUSION

     A candidate may lease a portion of a building owned by a corporation so long as the
lessee is the candidate's campaign committee, not the candidate himself.

FACTS

     Chris Mathys is the sole shareholder in ORO Financial, a corporation. ORO Financial
owns title to a building with office space. Mr. Mathys would like to use campaign funds to rent a
portion of the building for his campaign headquarters.




_________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-99-219
Page 2

ANALYSIS

Section 89517 describes the circumstances in which campaign funds may be used to
lease real property.² Subdivision (a) of that section provides that campaign funds shall not be
used for payment or reimbursement for the lease of real property where the lessee or sublessor is,
or the legal title resides, in whole or in part, in any of the following persons: a candidate, an
elected officer, a campaign treasurer, any individual or individuals with authority to approve the
expenditure of campaign funds, or a member of his or her immediate family.

Under this subdivision, the lessee or sublessor cannot be any of the persons indicated
above. However, there is no restriction if the lease or rental agreement is in the campaign
committee's name.

A lease arrangement is also prohibited if the legal title to the real property resides in
whole or in part in any of the persons listed above. You indicate that the legal title to the real
property is held by ORO Financial. The statute expressly prohibits a lease when the "legal title
resides" in a candidate. Even though Mr. Mathys is the sole shareholder in ORO Financial, he
does not hold legal title to the building. Therefore, a lease between Mr. Mathys' campaign
committee and ORO Financial is permissible under the Act.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By:    Deborah Allison
       Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:DA:tls

² Section 89517(b) prohibits the use of campaign funds to purchase real property.

QUESTION: May a candidate use campaign funds to lease a portion of a building for his or her
campaign headquarters when the building is owned by a corporation in which the candidate is the
sole shareholder?

CONCLUSION: A candidate may lease a portion of a building owned by a corporation so long as the
lessee is the candidate’s campaign committee, not the candidate himself.
